narrative_summary:
The BRCA1 missense change Val271Met (c.811G>A) occurs in the BRCA1 gene, a key susceptibility gene for hereditary breast and ovarian cancer (HBOC). The variant has been reported in multiple ethnic cohorts and in both germline and somatic contexts.

Clinical reports are heterogeneous. Several Japanese, Korean, Chinese and Taiwanese series have classified the alteration as a benign polymorphism or likely benign based on population frequency, lack of co‑segregation with disease and expert re‑interpretation (e.g., re‑classification as benign/likely benign in Japanese NGS screening [PMC4367084], Korean ovarian‑cancer cohort [PMC7593220], Chinese Hakka cohort [PMC9347172], amplicon‑NGS validation set [PMC7755770], Taiwanese general‑population study [PMC8256441], Australian familial‑cancer review [PMC11607406], Korean prostate‑cancer cohort [PMC9091831], Korean ACMG re‑classification study [PMC5654176] and a large Chinese Han screening [PMC10988807]). In contrast, a Brazilian at‑risk cohort described the same change as a variant of uncertain significance and noted its presence alongside pathogenic mutations, without definitive segregation data (VUS in Brazilian cohort [PMC5348334]). The Korean hereditary‑breast‑cancer study reported an odds ratio of 0.35 (95 % CI 0.07–1.93), indicating no clear association with disease risk [PMC8055990]. One report from an Asian multi‑gene panel labeled the variant as pathogenic in a single breast‑cancer case (deleterious classification) [PMC6078133], creating a direct conflict with the many benign/likely benign assignments.

No functional laboratory studies directly assessing the impact of Val271Met on BRCA1 protein function were identified in the extracted evidence.

Population data show the allele is rare but detectable in East Asian reference sets (e.g., gnomAD East Asian frequency ≈1.3 × 10⁻³ [PMC8256441]; 1000 Genomes East Asian frequency ≈1.32 × 10⁻³ [PMC8975122]). The Korean and Japanese cohort analyses reported low odds ratios and non‑significant risk estimates, supporting a lack of pathogenic effect [PMC8055990, PMC8975122]. Computational predictions are mixed: in silico tools (PolyPhen‑2, SIFT, Align‑GVGD, CADD) have assigned both possibly damaging and tolerated scores, with some databases listing the variant as a VUS or “probably neutral” (e.g., HGMD/ClinVar entries [PMC5348334]; CADD score 24 but classified as VUS [PMC5797818]; ACMG‑based computational criteria yielding benign supporting evidence [PMC5654176]).

Overall, the preponderance of evidence from multiple population‑based studies, expert re‑classifications, and the absence of functional data points toward BRCA1 Val271Met being a benign or likely benign polymorphism. However, isolated reports of uncertain significance, a single pathogenic classification, and inconsistent computational predictions introduce residual uncertainty, and the variant remains listed as a VUS in some clinical databases.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study lists the BRCA1 V271M variant as a polymorphism (benign) while also noting it as of uncertain significance in their cohort data.
article_mentions.title: Detection of BRCA1 and BRCA2 germline mutations in Japanese population using next-generation sequencing

mentions.quoted_text: Regarding missense variants, there was an extensive review of databases and in silico analysis, in order to classify those alterations according to its possible pathogenicity. As shown in Table 2, there is a large inconsistency among the databases considered for a large proportion of the variants analyzed. In addition, data regarding the frequency with which the change has been identified (number of families with the given alteration) and the co-segregation with proven pathogenic variants is reported. As detailed in Table 3, five variants were identified in patients carrying pathogenic mutations. The variants **c.811G>A** (BRCA1) and c.8187G>T (BRCA2) were identified in a patient carrying the pathogenic mutation c.8023A>G (p.Ile2675Val) at the BRCA2 gene. In addition, the alteration c.8351G>A (BRCA2) was identified in two patients and one of them is carrier of the mutation p.Arg337His at TP53 gene (this patient also has the variant c.7469T>C, at BRCA2 gene). Lastly, the alteration c.7448G>A (BRCA2) was identified in a patient carrying the BRCA1 mutation c.1687C>T(p.Gln563Ter).

Furthermore, three patients harbor more than one variant. One of them is carrier of the c.7469T>C (BRCA2) and c.8351G>A (BRCA2) variants (this patient also have the pathogenic mutation p.Arg337His at TP53 gene as described above); the other have the alterations c.4928T>C (BRCA2), and c.8351G>A (BRCA2). In addition one patient (with OC at 59 years of age and carrier of the pathogenic mutation c.8023A>G (p.Ile2675Val) at the BRCA2 gene) has two variants: **c.811G>A** (BRCA1) and c.8187G>T (BRCA2).

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study identifies the BRCA1 V271M (c.811G>A) variant as a variant of uncertain significance observed in a few individuals, often alongside other pathogenic mutations, and provides limited computational and clinical data without definitive pathogenicity classification.
article_mentions.title: Prevalence of BRCA1/BRCA2 mutations in a Brazilian population sample at-risk for hereditary breast cancer and characterization of its genetic ancestry

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The Korean hereditary breast cancer study re‑evaluated BRCA1/2 VUS, reporting that the BRCA1 V271M variant shows an odds ratio of 0.35 (95% CI 0.07‑1.93), indicating no clear pathogenic association.
article_mentions.title: Analysis of BRCA1/2 variants of unknown significance in the prospective Korean Hereditary Breast Cancer study

A 73-year-old woman with breast cancer was found to have BRCA1 **p.Val271Met** with C-score of 24. This variant was judged benign by Myriad (we had validation data with FALCO Biosystems)11 and uncertain by ClinVar. She had an aunt with breast cancer, a brother with pancreatic cancer and a daughter with ovarian cancer (Figure 2A). Her daughter with ovarian cancer had genetic counseling and testing showed the same variant. She suffers from recurrent serous adenocarcinoma of the ovary. Correlation of the variant and HBOC was suspected in this case. We need further follow up for this family.

Family trees of 3 patients with minor variants. (A) A 73-year-old woman with breast cancer was found to have BRCA1 **p.Val271Met** with C-score of 24. Her daughter with ovarian cancer had genetic counseling and testing showed the same variant. She suffered from recurrent serous adenocarcinoma of the ovary. B, A 45-year-old woman with breast cancer was found to have BRCA2 p.Lys322Gln with C-score of 16.89. This variant was identified in 6 patients with breast cancer. Her sister with breast cancer was referred to genetic counseling and testing showed her not to have the same variant. C, A 71-year-old woman with breast cancer had a variant of BRCA1 p.Met1628Thr with C-score of 0.023. This variant was not documented in the ClinVar database. Her daughter was referred to genetic counseling and testing. This variant was not found in the daughter, so the pathogenicity was defined to be low

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article uses CADD and other in‑silico scores to evaluate BRCA1/2 variants, classifying BRCA1 V271M as a VUS computationally, while clinical case data and a commercial laboratory label it benign, highlighting unresolved pathogenicity for this variant.
article_mentions.title: Combined annotation-dependent depletion score for BRCA1/2 variants in patients with breast and/or ovarian cancer

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study re‑evaluated BRCA1/2 VUS in Korean ovarian‑cancer patients and re‑classified the BRCA1 V271M (c.811G>A) variant as likely benign based on population frequency and functional evidence indicating it is not deleterious.
article_mentions.title: Reclassification of BRCA1 and BRCA2 variants found in ovarian epithelial, fallopian tube, and primary peritoneal cancers

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study reports that BRCA1/2 pathogenic mutations are frequent in Taiwanese ovarian cancer patients, while the specific BRCA1 V271M variant observed is classified as benign in the cohort's supplementary data.
article_mentions.title: Prevalence and clinical significance of BRCA1/2 germline and somatic mutations in Taiwanese patients with ovarian cancer

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article catalogs BRCA1/2 variants in the Taiwanese population and classifies the BRCA1 V271M variant as likely benign based on database annotations.
article_mentions.title: BRCA1 and BRCA2 Variation in Taiwanese General Population and the Cancer Cohort

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article classifies the BRCA1 V271M variant as benign based on ACMG/AMP criteria, noting some conflicting submissions in ClinVar.
article_mentions.title: Unrecognised actionability for breast cancer risk variants identified in a national-level review of Australian familial cancer centres

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study reports that male patients with BRCA-associated tumors have distinct clinicopathologic characteristics and that BRCA mutations, especially deleterious ones, affect overall and progression‑free survival across multiple cancer types.
article_mentions.title: Clinical characteristics and prognostic implications of BRCA-associated tumors in males: a pan-tumor survey

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study of germline variants in unselected Korean men with prostate cancer classified the BRCA1 V271M variant as likely benign.
article_mentions.title: Germline pathogenic variants in unselected Korean men with prostate cancer

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study reports a somatic BRCA1 V271M missense mutation among the cohort of kinase domain duplication patients, without further functional or pathogenicity interpretation.
article_mentions.title: Clinical and molecular characteristics of kinase domain duplications across diverse cancer types in the Chinese population

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study reclassifies Korean BRCA1/2 variants, identifying p.Leu1780Pro as a novel pathogenic founder mutation while classifying the BRCA1 Val271Met (V271M) variant as likely benign based on ACMG criteria, population odds ratios, and in silico predictions.
article_mentions.title: Identification of a Novel BRCA1 Pathogenic Mutation in Korean Patients Following Reclassification of BRCA1 and BRCA2 Variants According to the ACMG Standards and Guidelines Using Relevant Ethnic Controls

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study finds that germline pathogenic variants markedly increase breast cancer risk in Japanese women, though the specific V271M variant is only reported with frequency and prediction scores without a clear classification.
article_mentions.title: Association between germline pathogenic variants and breast cancer risk in Japanese women: The HERPACC study

article_mentions.title: Identification of Medically Actionable Secondary Findings in the 1000 Genomes

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The population‑based Chinese Han BRCA screening study reports a distinct mutational spectrum and identifies pathogenic/likely‑pathogenic carriers, while the specific BRCA1 V271M variant is classified as likely benign according to ACMG criteria in the supplementary data.
article_mentions.title: Population-based BRCA germline mutation screening in the Han Chinese identifies individuals at risk of BRCA mutation-related cancer: experience from a clinical diagnostic center from greater Shanghai area

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study reports that about 10% of Chinese renal cell carcinoma patients carry pathogenic or likely pathogenic germline mutations, including a BRCA1 V271M variant identified among the cohort.
article_mentions.title: Germline Mutation Landscape and Associated Clinical Characteristics in Chinese Patients With Renal Cell Carcinoma

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study develops a random‑forest model that predicts the pathogenicity of BRCA1 variants of unknown significance, achieving an AUC of 0.85 and applying it to over 6,300 BRCA1 variants, including V271M, to provide computational risk assessments.
article_mentions.title: Predicting Ovarian/Breast Cancer Pathogenic Risks of Human BRCA1 Gene Variants of Unknown Significance
